hero.headline: Implementing AI in finance, accounting and tax teams
hero.subheadline: We help finance teams move from AI experimentation to real adoption — integrating AI into everyday processes and workflows.
valuePoints: Deep understanding of finance, accounting and tax
Integrating AI into real financial processes

howWeHelp.title: How we help solve these situations
howWeHelp.subtitle:
Introducing AI in finance rarely starts with tools.
It starts with understanding processes, data, and how finance teams actually work.

That's why we follow a simple three-step approach.
howWeHelp.footer: Solutions that work in real finance workflows — not just in presentations.

steps.title: Identify opportunities
steps.headline: We find where AI can genuinely help in finance processes.
steps.conclusion: The result is a clear picture of where AI creates real value — and where it doesn't.

steps.title: Design the solution
steps.headline: We design how AI fits into everyday workflows.
steps.conclusion: The goal is to translate AI into practical steps in day-to-day work.

steps.title: Implementation and adoption
steps.headline: We help bring AI into real financial processes.
steps.conclusion: This ensures AI becomes part of everyday work — not just another experiment.

painPoints.title: Common situations when introducing AI into finance
painPoints.footer:
If any of these situations sound familiar, you're not alone.
Many finance teams are currently at a similar stage.

items.title: AI is already in use — but without governance
items.description: Employees use tools like ChatGPT or Copilot on personal accounts, while the company has no clear rules for handling sensitive financial data.

items.title: Licences exist — adoption doesn't
items.description: The company invested in AI tools, but finance teams rarely use them in their daily work.

items.title: Reporting is still manual
items.description: Monthly reporting is often built from ERP exports, Excel files, and manual checks.

items.title: Too much time spent on document work
items.description: Invoice checks, document reviews, and report commentary take up a large share of the finance team's time.

services.title: Our services
services.subtitle: We help companies introduce AI into financial processes — from the initial assessment to real use in day-to-day finance operations.
services.footer: Most engagements start with a short introductory workshop to identify the first meaningful opportunities for AI in financial processes.

items.title: AI Readiness for Finance
items.description: We identify where AI can bring real value to your financial processes.
items.conclusion: The result is a clear view of where AI creates real value — and where it doesn't.

items.title: AI Solution Design
items.description: We design a practical approach to integrating AI into the finance team's work.
items.conclusion: The goal is to translate AI into concrete steps in everyday processes.

items.title: AI Implementation and Adoption
items.description: We help bring AI into real financial operations.
bullets: support for adoption within the finance team
items.conclusion: This ensures AI becomes part of everyday work — not just another experiment.

servicesPage.title: What we do for you
servicesPage.subtitle: Every company is different. That's why we always start by understanding your specific context.

aboutPage.title: We understand finance from the inside.
aboutPage.subtitle: We've worked both in advisory firms and inside finance teams — which gives us a clear understanding of how change actually works in finance.

founder1.name: Lucie Fojtová
founder1.subtitle: Finance & Tax Transformation with AI
founder1.bio:
I started my career in Big4 consulting, where I learned what high-quality analysis and strategic advice should look like.

Later, I spent ten years working as an in-house tax manager in multinational companies — where recommendations must pass through corporate policies, legacy systems, fragmented data, and unclear ownership. The place where projects succeed or fail.

This experience allows me to bridge two worlds: advisory thinking and operational reality.

I am also a **certified tax advisor**.

Today, I focus on helping finance teams move from AI curiosity to real implementation — ensuring AI becomes part of everyday work rather than remaining a strategy document or a pilot project.

founder2.name: Renáta Kuchařová
founder2.subtitle: Finance Data & Analytics for AI-Driven Organizations
founder2.bio:
I started my career in Big4 audit, where I built a strong foundation in accounting and financial processes.

I later spent more than ten years working in finance teams in a technology company. I led a general ledger team and focused on financial data analysis and reporting.

This experience gave me a deep understanding of how finance operations and data structures interact.

Today, I help organizations use data, analytics, and AI to make finance teams more efficient and to support better decision-making.

whyUs.title: Why Next Change AI
items: Experience from both sides — Big4 advisory and in-house finance leadership
We know where AI creates real value and where it becomes hype
We focus on outcomes, not on selling tools
We understand finance processes, data, and operational reality
